compiled_state.title: [review-workflow]: light reviewer writes reviewer-findings.json to orchestrator artifacts dir instead of dispatched WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR -> overlay and re-review cannot find findings
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Sub-Agent Blocker

#### Expected Behavior

When `dso:code-reviewer-light` is dispatched with `WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR` set to a worktree-specific artifacts directory (e.g., `/tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122`), it should write `reviewer-findings.json` to that directory. Per `per-worktree-review-commit.md` Step 2: "tell the sub-agent to run `export WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR=<value>` as a Bash command before calling `write-reviewer-findings.sh`."

#### Actual Behavior

The light reviewer wrote `reviewer-findings.json` to the orchestrator's session artifacts directory (`/tmp/workflow-plugin-3e6d4b6cc62c8ab8`) instead of the dispatched worktree artifacts directory (`/tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122`). `record-review.sh` in the worktree context reported: `REVIEW BLOCKED: reviewer-findings.json not found`.

#### Dispatch Prompt Context (verbatim)
```
WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR: /tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122

## CRITICAL: First Bash call must be (all in ONE command):
export WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR="/tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122" && cd /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a33b50298047f5a1a && /Users/.../dso verify-review-diff.sh /tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122/review-diff-...txt

Then read the diff and perform your light-tier review. Write findings using write-reviewer-findings.sh with WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR exported.
```

Despite WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR being set in the prompt environment and first Bash call, the reviewer wrote to the orchestrator's artifacts dir. This occurred repeatedly across 3 separate dispatches.

#### Impact

Orchestrator cannot proceed to record-review.sh without manually copying findings from session artifacts dir to worktree artifacts dir. Workaround applied: `cp /tmp/workflow-plugin-3e6d4b6cc62c8ab8/reviewer-findings.json /tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122/reviewer-findings.json` (valid since hash verified matching).

#### Related

Pairs with the `WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR` requirement documented in `per-worktree-review-commit.md` Step 2 dispatch block. Root cause likely: `write-reviewer-findings.sh` calls `get_artifacts_dir()` which may not pick up the exported env var when the reviewer's Bash calls reset the exported env.
